items.prompt: Which set best matches advantages of crowdsourcing covered in this part?
choices: Cost-effectiveness, speed and scale, diverse perspectives, flexibility
Guaranteed perfect labels with no validation needed
items.explain: Section 9.2.4 lists cost, speed/scalability, diversity, and flexibility as advantages.

items.prompt: Without oversight, errors or biases can enter crowd submissions and harm consistency.
items.explain: Quality control is a central challenge when many individuals complete tasks.

items.prompt: What are gold-standard questions used for in crowdsourcing?
choices: Embedding known-answer items to check response accuracy
Encrypting all worker devices at rest
items.explain: Best practices include gold-standard validation to maintain quality and reduce errors.

items.prompt: Name one ethical concern about crowd worker pay or working conditions.
items.rubric: Accept fair pay, low cents-per-task rates, exploitation, lack of benefits/security, or precarious microtask labor.
items.sample_answer: Many workers receive very low pay and lack job security or benefits, raising fair-compensation and exploitation concerns.

items.prompt: Sharing sensitive data with crowd workers can raise security, privacy, and regulatory compliance issues.
items.explain: Challenges and ethics sections both flag third-party handling of sensitive data and rules such as GDPR.
